topic: Forensic Zoom chart proves κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via L1A induction-residual analysis
change_summary: ISS-2076 self-applied: Butterworth 1930 citation corrected. Bad DOI rspa.1930.0204 removed; journal label flipped from 'Proc. R. Soc. A 7:536–541' to 'Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541'. Wikipedia overview link preserved.
our_argument_summary: WIN-070 attacks the dome home page's 'Forensic Zoom' chart on five independent grounds (impossible L1A+µGal unit pairing, non-existent 'Induction Residual' product name, orbital-mechanics contradiction with a 27-minute BOU phase-lock, missing GRACE parser in the dome repo, missing AI formula) plus a sixth on Exhibit B's Butterworth-mask unfalsifiability. Verdict: Refuted by Data, anchored by the orbital-mechanics argument.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No change since c2 — the Forensic Zoom chart is a static HTML artifact with no live data fetch or comparison logic. monitor.py contains a single static GRACE-FO CSR citation in win text; no GRACE L1A parser, no µGal computation, no Asymmetry-Index estimator anywhere in the dome repository.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 are static HTML strings; no script in the repo computes either from any GRACE or BOU data.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET BGS GIN (for the BOU magnetometer top panel — the only authentic data product)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Exhibit B's 'Level-1B Administrative Mask' framing functions as an unfalsifiability shield — every public dataset that lacks the spike is interpreted as confirming the mask; any that shows it is interpreted as confirming the cavity. The Butterworth-mask physics claim (now correctly cited) makes a physically wrong promise about the filter's behavior.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. Our review's code claims remain accurate after the Stage 2 EXP-423 prose patch and the ISS-2076 citation correction.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c2. monitor.py has only a static GRACE-FO CSR citation, no fetch, no compare. The Forensic Zoom chart is a static HTML artifact. 'none' remains correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c2. Standard physics gravito-magnetic coupling is ~10^−21 weaker than κ = 1.67 nT/µGal — the dome is making a novel-mechanism claim, not relabeling a standard phenomenon.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c2. The 2003 Halloween storm BOU magnetometer data has been in INTERMAGNET archives since 2003; the κ value was claimed in earlier dome versions (see WIN-012, WIN-058b); the Forensic Zoom chart and its certifying statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) were assembled in the V51 era (2026) to retrospectively demonstrate the already-claimed value. The borderline-rationale concern (c1 hole #5 → c2 hole #3 → still open here) remains: this WIN attacks a chart presentation rather than a prospective prediction, so the tag's strict definition pulls slightly. See holes_found[2] below.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c2. The chart attempts to derive κ from an empirical correlation of two time series, not from dome geometric parameters. WIN-058b establishes κ is not derived from dome geometry at all.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c2 hole #1 (still open after ISS-2076 patch): the first leg of the inline temporal-rescue closure in point (3) — 'phase-locked is the dome\'s own term and denotes a spatial-temporal coupling — a strictly-temporal rescue therefore contradicts the chart\'s headline framing' — still depends on the dome's chart text using 'phase-locked' in a sense that necessarily implies spatial co-location. A careful dome defender could rebut: 'in our usage, phase-locked just means cross-correlation peaks at zero lag; we never claimed spatial colocation.' The wrap-up paragraph does later refer to 'phase-locked space–ground correlation' but that is our characterization of the dome's claim, not a verbatim dome quote. Per c2 hole #1: the second leg (telemetry-timing reconciliation) is independent and robust, so the rescue still closes; this remains a presentational sharpening rather than a structural failure. The ISS-2076 patch did not touch this point — it only corrected the Butterworth citation in point (6), so the carry-over status is expected and the patch did not regress it.
holes_found.recommendation: Same as c2 hole #1: either (a) anchor the spatial-temporal-coupling claim in a verbatim dome chart-caption quote (if the chart caption contains 'space-ground' or similar wording at dome SHA c8eaeb1b), or (b) soften the first leg to 'phase-locked at minimum denotes temporal phase alignment, which itself fails the L1A telemetry-timing reconciliation argued below.' Not blocking. If the decider has already accepted the existing wording in c2 review without patching, recording wontfix-with-rationale would close this carry-over cleanly.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c2 hole #2 (still open after ISS-2076 patch): in the inline temporal-rescue closure of point (3), the phrase 'even the strictly-temporal reading at 1-second cadence over a 27-minute window' still uses '1-second cadence' to describe the chart's display resolution. GRACE accelerometer Level-1A (ACC1A) is sampled at 10 Hz (0.1-second cadence), so the 1-second cadence is the chart's display rate, not the GRACE native sampling rate. A reader cross-checking against JPL D-22027 (cited in the same sentence) will notice the glide-over. The ISS-2076 patch was scoped to point (6) only and correctly did not touch point (3), so the carry-over status is expected.
holes_found.recommendation: Same as c2 hole #2: reword to 'even the strictly-temporal reading requires explicit telemetry-timing reconciliation between the chart\'s 1-second-cadence display and the L1A epoch tags, 10-Hz accelerometer sampling, and downsampling schedule documented in JPL D-22027.' One-phrase sharpening; not blocking. As with hole #0, wontfix-with-rationale would also close cleanly if the decider judges the current wording acceptable.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c1 hole #5 → c2 hole #3 (still open after ISS-2076 patch): the post_hoc:true tag's rationale in code_analysis_tags remains borderline for a WIN that attacks a chart presentation rather than a prospective prediction. The dome's κ = 1.67 nT/µGal value is shared across WIN-011/012/058b and predates the Forensic Zoom chart; the chart is a retroactive presentational artifact. ISS-2076 was a citation fix in detail_evidence point (6) and did not touch code_analysis_tags, so the carry-over status is expected. This is the THIRD consecutive cycle this hole remains open without an associated open-issue ISS — decider should either patch the rationale, flip the tag, or close as wontfix-with-rationale to retire the carry-over.
holes_found.recommendation: Same as c1/c2: either tighten post_hoc_evidence to clarify 'post_hoc applies to the Forensic Zoom chart construction (assembled in V51 [2026] to retrospectively demonstrate κ), not to the κ value itself (which is evaluated under post-hoc framing in WIN-012/058b),' or flip the tag to false on the rationale that this WIN evaluates a chart's evidentiary failure rather than a prospective prediction. Three-cycle carry-over without an ISS is a process smell — recommend explicit decider action this round (patch, flip, or wontfix-with-rationale) to retire the loop.

stronger_arguments.description: Unchanged from c2: the dome's own claim_index.json (per c1 analyst note) classifies the κ family as 'pending_contested' status. Quoting the dome's own internal classification of κ as contested undercuts any defender claim that κ = 1.67 nT/µGal is settled within the dome model itself.
sources: dome repo claim_index.json (per c1 analyst note)

stronger_arguments.description: Unchanged from c2: the 10^21× scale mismatch between dome κ and gravito-magnetic effects in mainstream physics is a quantitative anchor that closes the 'novel mechanism' rescue without invoking presentation-level failures.
sources: Standard gravitomagnetism scale (Mashhoon 2003); compared to dome κ = 1.67 nT/µGal

stronger_arguments.description: Unchanged from c2: WIN-034 (Solar System symmetry) provides an independent attack surface on κ — the symmetry argument constrains any cavity-coupling mechanism without needing to engage with the Forensic Zoom exhibit's presentational failures.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c2 and consistent with this run's verification (data/wins.json:070 detail_evidence kernel paragraph). The top-panel BOU magnetometer trace reproduces real INTERMAGNET data for the Oct 30 2003 Halloween storm at Boulder (data/wins.json:070 cites imag-data.bgs.ac.uk/GIN_V1/GINServices). The ±20–23 nT excursion at mid-latitude during Dst = −383 nT is consistent with published storm-time literature (Pulkkinen et al. 2005, doi:10.1029/2005JA011090, cited in detail_extra). The dome did the easy part right.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Borrowing real data on the top panel does not validate a fabricated bottom panel. The κ = 1.67 nT/µGal claim depends on the bottom panel's 'L1A Induction Residual (µGal)' product and its certifying statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152), all of which fail on independent grounds (data/wins.json:070 detail_evidence points 1–5, plus the now-correctly-cited Exhibit B unfalsifiability argument in point 6). The kernel covers the magnetometer-side authenticity but does not extend to anything below it.

deeper_analysis: This c3 review is a narrow verification of the ISS-2076 self-applied patch and confirmation that no other content drifted. The patch closes the c2 MAJOR cleanly: the bad DOI 10.1098/rspa.1930.0204 is gone, the journal label is now 'Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng.' (correct), and the Wikipedia overview link is preserved as the testable anchor for the physics claim that a Butterworth low-pass does not erase impulsive transients. Verbatim current text: '...(Butterworth filter overview; Butterworth 1930, Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541).' This is the form c2 recommended (option (a) — corrected journal, no DOI). Considering the other side: a dome defender could still argue that a primary citation to a journal a reader cannot easily access (Exp. Wireless and the Wireless Engineer is a 1930 trade journal, not indexed on common DOI databases) is no better than the Wikipedia link alone. That defense is rhetorically real but our text already preserves the Wikipedia link as the actually-clickable anchor, so the primary cite is a credit-and-context entry rather than a load-bearing reference. The physics claim does not require the 1930 paper to be checkable — standard texts (Oppenheim & Schafer §7.2) document Butterworth filter behavior in modern form. No regression, no new hole introduced by the patch. The remaining three minor carry-overs (c2 holes #1, #2, #3) are not regressed by the patch — they are pre-existing presentational sharpenings unrelated to the Butterworth citation scope. The detail_evidence length dropped 42 chars (8482 → 8440) consistent with removing the DOI URL + href + extra journal letters net, confirming a small surgical edit.

citations_verified: Butterworth 1930, Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541 — correctly attributed journal (Wikipedia 'Butterworth filter' overview names this as Butterworth's original publication; PRSA vol 7 = 1854–1857, ruling out the c2 misattribution definitively)
Pulkkinen et al. 2005, doi:10.1029/2005JA011090 — unchanged from c2; verified in c1
INTERMAGNET BGS GIN URL — unchanged from c2; verified in c1
JPL D-22027 (PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf) — unchanged from c2; verified in c1
citations_unchecked: JPL D-32865 (backup link) — verified in c1, no change
PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 landing page — verified in c1, no change

summary_for_decider: WIN-070 c3 narrow verification of ISS-2076 self-apply. RESULT: patch landed cleanly — bad DOI 10.1098/rspa.1930.0204 removed, journal corrected to 'Exp. Wireless & Wireless Eng. 7:536–541', Wikipedia link preserved. No regression, no new holes introduced. Verdict holds: Refuted by Data. Three minor carry-over holes (c2 #1 phase-locked spatial assumption, c2 #2 1s-vs-10Hz cadence, c1#5/c2#3 post_hoc tag rationale) remain open but were out of ISS-2076's scope, so carry-over status is expected. PROCESS FLAG: the post_hoc tag rationale carry-over is now in its THIRD consecutive cycle without an open-issue ISS. Recommend the decider this round either (a) patch one or more of the carry-overs, or (b) close them as wontfix-with-rationale to retire the loop. PROP-009: queue_id=377, queue_pushed_at=2026-05-17T18:24:04Z — both populated. Singleton run (only one un-reviewed queue item present; queue_id 376 was already reviewed in RW-002-integration.json). Tests not run from curmudgeon side (read-only role); decider's pre-push gate will catch any test failures. Considering the other side as the operator prefers: a dome defender could argue the corrected journal is still functionally inaccessible (a 1930 trade publication is hard to fetch), so the credibility-restoration value is partial — but the Wikipedia anchor already does the actually-clickable work, so this critique does not reopen the hole.
